raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.0,
    "R1.2": 0.0,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.0,
    "R1.5": 0.0,
    "R1.6": 0.0,
    "R1.7": 1.0,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.3", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.3": "Verifying citations entails checking not only that cited works exist but also that claims attributed to them are accurate representations of what those works show — e.g., confirming that 'Kowalski et al. (2019)' genuinely introduced COMET and demonstrated the stated sensitivity result (a plausible error given that Rei et al. 2020 is separately cited as the COMET source). This dimension is central but drops out entirely under the narrow bibliographic-only interpretation, making it the hinge of the ambiguity.",
    "R1.7": "The delegation directly and exclusively requests citation checking; reference accuracy, author/year correctness, existence of cited works, and attribution fidelity are the explicit load-bearing tasks. Six distinct citations are present, including one (Kowalski et al., 2019 for COMET) that conflicts with another in-text attribution (Rei et al., 2020)."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should I verify only bibliographic accuracy (correct authors, years, and existence of the cited works), or should I also assess whether each in-text claim faithfully represents what the cited work actually demonstrates?"
}

rationale.R1.3: Verifying citations entails checking not only that cited works exist but also that claims attributed to them are accurate representations of what those works show — e.g., confirming that 'Kowalski et al. (2019)' genuinely introduced COMET and demonstrated the stated sensitivity result (a plausible error given that Rei et al. 2020 is separately cited as the COMET source). This dimension is central but drops out entirely under the narrow bibliographic-only interpretation, making it the hinge of the ambiguity.
rationale.R1.7: The delegation directly and exclusively requests citation checking; reference accuracy, author/year correctness, existence of cited works, and attribution fidelity are the explicit load-bearing tasks. Six distinct citations are present, including one (Kowalski et al., 2019 for COMET) that conflicts with another in-text attribution (Rei et al., 2020).

parsed.clarification_question: Should I verify only bibliographic accuracy (correct authors, years, and existence of the cited works), or should I also assess whether each in-text claim faithfully represents what the cited work actually demonstrates?
